program_chi: 社會工作社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Social Work 1. Major
Programme: The Social Work Programme
prepares students to be professionally competent social workers. At the end
of the course of studies, students will have: (1) possessed basic knowledge and
understanding of the values of Social Work (K/V); (2) identified with the humanistic values
and had a genuine concern for people especially for the vulnerable groups
(V); (3) had commitment to the well-being of
clients, and to social justice (V); (4) understood the social, cultural and
psychological factors contributing to the needs and problems of people (K/S); (5) been equipped with knowledge and
skills on social work research, administration and policy (K/S); and (6) been equipped with knowledge and
skills in working with individual, group and community/society (K/S). V =
Value K =
Knowledge S = Skill 2. Minor
Programmes: (i) Minor
in Social Welfare (ii) Minor
in Social Policy Upon the completion of the Minor
Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental
issues related to the subject, acquired knowledge of some aspects of the
subject with sufficient exposure and at a suitable level to allow further
study in this subject or related subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Social Work Social Work Applicable to
students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete
a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
SOWK1001 and any two courses,
offered by two different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the
following:
ARCH1001, 1002, 1003, COMM1110, 1120,
1150, 1500, DSPS1001, 1003, 1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020,
1076, 1077, GRMD1302, 1401, 1402, PSYC1000, 1630, SOCI1001, 1201,
SOSC1001, 1002, 1003, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  51
SOWK2001, 2020, 2110, 2140, 2210,
2220, 2230, 2430, 3310, 3320, 3340, 3410, 3420, 4020 (capstone course),
4350, 4410, 4420
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  at least one course from SOWK2050,
3240, 3250  |  3
(b)  |  at least three courses from SOWK4030
(capstone course)[a], 4510, 4520, 4530, 4540, 4550, 4570, 4580, 4590, 4591,
4592  |  9
Total:  |  72
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  SOWK courses at 2000 and above level
will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
2.  |  For students transferred to the
Social Work Programme and who had taken SOWK1113 or 1114 as part of the Faculty
Package, the courses should be counted as part of their elective courses. They
should take an additional Faculty Package course to fulfill the Faculty
Package requirement.
3.  |  Only a number of seminar courses
will be offered in each academic year in accordance with resources
available.
4.  |  All social work students are
required to take at least one PSYC course and one SOCI course, including
the ones listed under the Faculty Package.
[a]  |  Students are encouraged to take
SOWK4030 preferably in their final year of study to enhance their research
ability by conducting a guided research on selected topics.

Minor Programme Title Social Policy
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 18 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  9
SOWK1114, 3320, 3340
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  9
Any 9 units from the following:
SOWK2160, 2200, 2202, 2203, 2204, 3250, 4510, 4520,
4530, 4550, 4592
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  “Social Policy” and “Social Welfare” are two
independent Minor Programmes. Students are required to indicate their
choice to the Department when applying. On completion of the unit requirement,
the name of the chosen Minor Programme will be shown on the transcript.
Minor Programme Title Social Welfare
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 18 units of courses as follows:
Units

SOWK1001, 3310, 3320
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  9
Any 9 units from the following:
SOWK1130, 1150, 1160, 2020, 2110, 2140, 4540
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Note:
1.  |  “Social Policy” and “Social Welfare” are two
independent Minor Programmes. Students are required to indicate their
choice to the Department when applying. On completion of the unit requirement,
the name of the chosen Minor Programme will be shown on the transcript.
